title: From Living in a Closet to YC’s Fastest-Growing AI Startup

source.description:
What if the safest career move right now… is quitting your “safe” job?

Connor (Co-Founder of Hyperspell) was rejected by YC six times.
Lived in a closet in San Francisco.
Refunded paying customers to start over from zero.

Today, Hyperspell is one of the fastest-growing YC companies — building memory infrastructure for AI agents in a world where digital workers may soon outnumber humans.

In this episode of Talks With Dasha, we go beyond startup hype and talk about:
• Why most startups fail (and it’s not the idea)
• Shipping a critical bug fix 20 minutes before a YC interview
• Why AI without memory is a “clueless genius”
• Why stable jobs might be riskier than building something new
• The future of AI agents replacing digital busywork
• Why soft skills will dominate in an AI-first world
• “No serious dating until Series B” 👀

This is a conversation about obsession, resilience, and building before you’re obvious.

If you care about:

AI
Startups
Y Combinator
Founder mindset
Building from zero
Or the future of work

You’re in the right place.

Subscribe for more conversations with emerging founders shaping the next decade of tech — before the headlines catch up.

🌐 Learn more about Hyperspell: https://www.hyperspell.com

🔗 Connect with Conor on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/conorbrennanburke/

About the host

Dasha Shunina treats every interview like an angel investment — she only sits down with founders she genuinely believes in or deeply admires.

She is the creator and host of Talks With Dasha, founder of Women Tech Meetup (10,000+ female founders and operators), a Venture Capital Contributor at Forbes, and she also leads Go-To-Market  at Puzzle.

Through conversations, curated events, and media, Dasha spotlights emerging founders before they become obvious.

Connect with Dasha:
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dashashunina/
X: https://x.com/dasha_shunina
Instagram: @feed_my_unicorn

24:53 Soft skills are the new hard skills
26:16 Selling to AI agents instead of humans

47:07 Why they’re great co-founders
48:39 Closing thoughts

theme.summary: This transcript is fundamentally focused on entrepreneurship, business strategy, and startup development. The entire conversation covers Connor's entrepreneurial journey building Hyperspell, extensively discussing core business concepts including: startup founding and scaling, customer-first business strategy, product-market fit, fundraising and YC application process, business model development for AI infrastructure, go-to-market strategies, co-founder relationships, business decision-making under uncertainty, and practical startup advice. While it touches on AI and technology (memory for AI agents), these are explored through the lens of business strategy and building a successful company. The discussion covers practical business advice on everything from customer onboarding to sales strategies to organizational building. This aligns perfectly with the existing business category which already contains extensive content on entrepreneurship, startup strategy, business models, venture capital, and company building frameworks from other successful founders and business leaders.
